steps.title: Keep preposition and object together
steps.content: The phrase needs of all the elements on the periodic table. A preposition should not be separated from its object by punctuation.

steps.title: Reject punctuation breaks
steps.content: Choice B inserts an em dash, Choice C inserts a comma, and Choice D inserts a colon between of and its object. Each break makes the sentence ungrammatical.

steps.title: Use plain of
steps.content: Choice A leaves of directly before its object, producing the standard phrase highest melting point of all the elements. The answer is A.
